title: IREN Prices Upsized $2.6 Billion Convertible Notes Offering
text:
IREN Limited announced the pricing of convertible senior notes totaling $2.6 billion, representing an increase from the initially planned $2 billion offering. The notes carry 1.00% convertible senior notes due 2033 with a conversion premium of 32.5%.

Key transaction elements include an initial conversion price of approximately $73.07 per share, based on the prior day's closing price of $55.15. The firm also entered into capped call transactions with an initial cap price of $110.30 per share to hedge potential share dilution upon conversion.

The offering was scheduled to settle on May 14, 2026. IREN estimated net proceeds of approximately $2.57 billion (potentially $2.96 billion if underwriters exercised their option for an additional $400 million in notes). The company planned to allocate roughly $174.5 million toward capped call transaction costs, with remaining funds designated for general corporate purposes and working capital.
